en.value: paper used for handwriting, printed with squares, typically 200 or 400 per sheet, each square designed to accommodate a single character or punctuation mark, used in Japan, South Korea, and Taiwan

value.text: ① Title on 1st column, 1st character in 4th square ② Author name on 2nd column; 1 square between surname and given name; 1 empty square below ③ 1st sentence begins on 3rd column, 2nd square; new paragraph begins on 2nd square ④ Subheadings have 1 empty column before/after and begin on 3rd square of new column ⑤ Punctuation marks have their own square, except at the end of column; then they share a square with last character
